content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 25, 1994

David O. Levine
10347 Tennessee Avenue
Los Angeles, California 90064

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-330

Dear Mr. Levine:

We write in response to your October 7, 1994, request for
conflict-of-interest advice under the Political Reform Act (the
"Act") which encompasses Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

The Act regulates the conduct of public officials. Public
officials include every member, officer, and employee or
consultant, as defined in Commission regulations, of a state or
local government agency. (Section 82048.) As you are a private
employee, albeit of a public official, your conduct is not
regulated under the Act. Consequently, there can be no conflict
of interest as defined in Section 87100 of the Act, based on your
personal investments.

As you note in your letter, your employer, Mr. Epstein, will
receive no financial interest in your investment. Therefore, the
facts raised in your request do not present a possible conflict
for your employer. We have enclosed some general information on
conflict of interest laws administered by our agency.

We trust that this brief response adequately addresses the
issues identified in your request. Should you have additional
questions regarding operation of the Act, please feel free to
contact the undersigned at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
